div.head: The Secretary of War (Patterson) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Dear Mr. Secretary: I acknowledge receipt of the joint letter from the Foreign Economic Administration and the State Department dated September 20, 1945, setting forth the terms and conditions of payment for the issuance of maintenance items for United States equipment now in the possession of Allied Forces, under paragraph c of the Presidential Policy on Military Lend-Lease contained in JCS 771/18, dated September 5, 1945.Not printed here.

I am enclosing herewith a copy of the War Department directiveNot printed. issued to the Commanding General, Army Service Forces,Lt. Gen. Brehon B. Somervell. Commanding General, Army Air Forces,General of the Army Henry H. Arnold. and to all Theater Commanders.

The War Department will render separate reports for billing purposes to the Foreign Economic Administration or its designated successor organization covering all transfers made under this authority and require that all requisitions accepted from foreign governments bear the statement: “It is agreed that full cash payment will be made upon presentation of a bill by the United States Government.”

The War Department takes the position that it may transfer reasonable quantities of materials without any limitations as to total value of aid furnished under this arrangement. Further, such aid will be rendered to all countries which have received Lend-Lease aid unless otherwise specifically instructed by the State Department.Information concerning the furnishing of maintenance items in return for cash payment was communicated in a note from the Secretary of State to the British Ambassador on October 12, not printed. For text of note sent, mutatis mutandis, on the same date to the Soviet Chargé, see vol. v, p. 1042.
